patent_context.patent_title: Modular solar panel mounting system with adaptive tilt mechanism
patent_context.claim_description: A modular solar panel mounting system comprising a base frame assembly, an adaptive tilt mechanism arranged to adjust panel angle, a wind load sensor unit, and a control apparatus configured to optimise energy capture

claims.claim_text: A modular solar panel mounting system comprising a base frame assembly, an adaptive tilt mechanism arranged to adjust panel angle, a wind load sensor unit, and a control apparatus configured to optimise energy capture

structured_elements.text: adaptive tilt mechanism arranged to adjust panel angle
control apparatus configured to optimise energy capture

claims.accused_product_description: The SunTrack Lite is a solar panel mounting rail with a fixed tilt bracket. It has no motorised components, no wind sensors, and no electronic control system. Tilt angle is set manually during installation.

element_mapping_facts.e1: SunTrack Lite is a solar panel mounting system — matched
element_mapping_facts.e2: Mounting rail with bracket — may or may not qualify as a 'base frame assembly'
element_mapping_facts.e3: Fixed tilt bracket, manual adjustment only — unclear if 'adaptive tilt mechanism' requires motorised or automatic adjustment
element_mapping_facts.e5: No electronic control system — unclear if manual installation angle selection could ever be a 'control apparatus configured to optimise'

claims.disputed_element_notes: The term 'adaptive tilt mechanism' is disputed — does it require automated or motorised adjustment, or could a manually adjustable bracket suffice? The term 'control apparatus configured to optimise' is also highly ambiguous — the accused product has no electronics. The 'base frame assembly' term could be read broadly to include any mounting rail or narrowly as a specific structural assembly.
claims.specification_notes: The specification describes a motorised actuator for the tilt mechanism and a microcontroller-based control apparatus with software optimisation algorithms.
